case_name: Samuel C. Arp, II v. Indiana State Police

summary.brief: Samuel C. Arp, II v. Indiana State Police. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Samuel C. Arp II was an employee of the Indiana State Police (ISP), first as a state trooper, and then as an employee on disability status after he was injured in the line of duty.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Arp began receiving long-term disability (LTD) benefits pursuant to the terms of a Pension Trust Agreement (the Trust) and Supplemental Trust Agreement (the Supplemental Trust).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In 2006, Arp sustained extensive back injuries when he was struck by a drunk driver while on duty.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Arp’s last regular workday with ISP was September 23, 2007; he was subsequently classified as disabled.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In 2012, Arp notified ISP of his intention to attend law school.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In 2014, Major Charles Sorrells, ISP’s Assistant Chief of Staff of Human Resources and Administration, sent correspondence to Arp.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Arp began his legal career with the Vanderburgh County Prosecutor’s Office.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Arp notified Captain Michael Wylie of his candidacy for elected prosecutor of Lawrence County.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Reinstatement of Arp’s LTD benefits is not capable of being enforced.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Legal Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation regarding employment and disability benefits

reveal.result_summary: Consultation with ISP legal division regarding employment status
reveal.decision_impact: Impacts decision to pursue elected office

timeline_phases.name: Initial Employment and Injury
timeline_phases.description: Arp's employment with ISP and subsequent injury

timeline_phases.name: Decision to Run for Office
timeline_phases.description: Arp's decision to run for elected office while on disability benefits

decision_points.clinical_context: Decision to run for elected office while on disability benefits
decision_points.prompt: Should Arp have resigned from ISP before assuming elected office?

options.description: Resign from ISP before assuming office
options.clinical_reasoning: Required by ISP regulations and Indiana law

options.description: Do not resign and assume office
options.clinical_reasoning: Contrary to ISP regulations and Indiana law

actual_action_defendant.description: Arp did not resign before assuming office

expected_action_court.description: Arp should have resigned from ISP
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Mandated by Regulation 6 and Indiana Code

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to comply with statutory and regulatory requirements
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Arp should have resigned to comply with legal obligations

key_findings: Arp was not entitled to continued employment with ISP
LTD benefits terminated upon separation from ISP

factual_timeline.event: Arp's last regular workday with ISP

allegations.allegation: Failure to resign before assuming office
Entitlement to continued employment and LTD benefits

court_findings.finding: Arp was required to resign before assuming office
Arp not entitled to specific performance
